zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Joyce Cornwall於1414年出生在英國史洛普爾斯塔普萊頓時，她的父親Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burford55歲，母親Cecilia Alice Merbury50歲。她於1429年與Richard Leighton結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1432年在英國史洛普爾享年18歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾布福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Joyce Cornwall于1414年出生在英国史洛普爾斯塔普萊頓时，她的父亲Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burford55岁，母亲Cecilia Alice Merbury50岁。她于1429年与Richard Leighton结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1432年在英国史洛普爾享年18岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾布福德。

nameMeanings.name: Cornwall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Corney
meanings: habitational name from Corney in Cumbria or Corney in Hertfordshire (now known as Corneybury), from Old English corn ‘grain’ or corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + ēg ‘island’. It seems possible, from the distribution of early forms, that it may also derive from a lost place in Lancashire. Alternatively, a habitational name from Corney (Lancashire), later named Corneyrow, now Corner Row or Cornoe in Greenhalgh (alias Greenall), in the parish of Kirkham. Corney was sometimes pronounced and spelled Cornah, Corner, Cornall, Gorney, and Gornall.
perhaps a shortened form of   Cornwall   or   Cornish  , or a metathesized form of   Croney  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Joyce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Apparently from the Norman male name Josce (Middle English Josse), which in turn is from Jodocus, a Latinized form of a Breton name, Iodoc, meaning ‘lord’, borne by a 7th-century Breton saint. The name was in use in England among Breton followers of William the Conqueror. However, although this was fairly common as a male given name in the Middle Ages, it had virtually died out by the 14th century. There is evidence of its use as a girl's name from the 16th century onwards in parishes with strong Puritan links, which suggests that it may have been associated with the vocabulary word joy; see Joy . It was strongly revived in the 19th century under the influence of popular fiction. It is borne by characters in Mrs Henry Wood's East Lynne ( 1861 ) and Edna Lyall's In the Golden Days ( 1885 ). Modern use may well have been influenced also by the common Irish surname derived from the medieval Norman male name. See also Joss .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Joyce Cornwall was born in 1414, in Stapleton, Shropshire, England, her father, Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burford, was 55 and her mother, Cecilia Alice Merbury, was 50. She married Richard Leighton in 1429. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1432, in Shropshire, England, at the age of 18, and was buried in Burford, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Joyce Cornwall est née en 1414, à Stapleton, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burford, avait 55 ans et sa mère, Cecilia Alice Merbury, avait 50 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Leighton en 1429. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1432, à Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 18 ans, et a été enterrée à Burford, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Joyce Cornwall 1414 in Stapleton, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burford 55 und ihre Mutter Cecilia Alice Merbury 50. Sie hat 1429 Richard Leighton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1432 im Alter von 18 Jahren in Shropshire, England und wurde in Burford, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Joyce Cornwall è nata il nel 1414 nel Stapleton, Shropshire, England, sua padre, Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burford, aveva 55 anni e sua madre, Cecilia Alice Merbury, aveva 50 anni. Lei sposato Richard Leighton nel 1429. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1432, nel Shropshire, England, all'età di 18 anni, e fu sepolta a Burford, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Joyce Cornwallが1414年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアスタプレトンで生まれた時，父Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burfordは55歳，母Cecilia Alice Merburyは50歳であった。1429年にRichard Leightonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1432年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで18歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアブルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Joyce Cornwall(이)가 1414년 영국 슈럽실르 스타플레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burford(은)는 55세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecilia Alice Merbury(은)는 50세였다. 그녀는 1429년 Richard Leighton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1432년 영국 슈럽실르에서 18세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 버퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Joyce Cornwall nasceu em 1414, em Stapleton, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burford, tinha 55 anos e sua mãe, Cecilia Alice Merbury, tinha 50 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Leighton em 1429. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1432, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 18 anos, e foi sepultada em Burford, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Joyce Cornwall родилась в 1414 г. в местности стаплетон, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burford было 55 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecilia Alice Merbury, 50. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Leighton в 1429 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1432 г. в местности шропшир, Англия в возрасте 18 лет и была похоронена в местности бурфорд, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Joyce Cornwall nació en 1414, en Stapleton, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Cornwall 5th Baron Burford, tenía 55 años y su mamá, Cecilia Alice Merbury, tenía 50 años. Se casó con Richard Leighton en 1429. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1432, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 18 años, y fue sepultada en Burford, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
